Tests that `Math.sin(NaN)` returns `NaN` using an assertion.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the behavior of the `Math.sin` function when it receives `NaN` (Not-a-Number) as an input. The `assert` statement checks that the result of `Math.sin(NaN)` is also `NaN`, which is the expected behavior according to the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing solely on validating the correct handling of `NaN` by the `Math.sin` function.
